medical_history.chief_complaint: Abnormal ocular movements, walking disorder with frequent falls and language difficulties
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: At age 3 years, parents noticed abnormal ocular movements, walking disorder with frequent falls and language difficulties. Clinically diagnosed with cerebellar ataxia. Cranial MRI scans showed cerebellar atrophy. At age 6 years, referred to Neuropaediatrics unit. Neurological exam showed oculomotor apraxia, ataxic gait and dysmetria. Also presented distal limb dystonia, bilateral spontaneous Babinski and rotulian and achillean hyporeflexia. Vitamin E levels were low at age 7 years (1.3 μg/ml and 0.4 μg/ml; normal range 3–15 μg/ml). Vitamin E levels returned to normal (11.7 μg/ml) after oral vitamin E treatment. EMG at age 7 years showed a discrete reduction of the sural sensory nerve action potential (SNAP) amplitude. At age 9 years, EMG results were in agreement with sensitive axonal polyneuropathy.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Cerebellar ataxia, oculomotor apraxia, dysmetria, distal limb dystonia, hyporeflexia, sensitive axonal polyneuropathy
medical_history.family_history: Healthy non-consanguineous Moroccan parents. Two healthy brothers. No family history of interest.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological exam showed oculomotor apraxia, ataxic gait and dysmetria. Also presented distal limb dystonia, bilateral spontaneous Babinski and rotulian and achillean hyporeflexia.
